details.behavior: Message sent via input, Chat API called but FAILED with net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. Text remains in input box. No error message, no warning, no response shown in UI. Silent failure — user sees nothing changed.
console_errors: Failed to load resource: net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR — /apiv2/kimi.gateway.chat.v1.ChatService/Chat:1 (2nd attempt)
details.ui_state_after_block: Text stays in input box, KIMI logo remains in center, no chat messages appear, no error toast/modal
details.analysis: The kimi_connect template sends a 400 Bad Request with Connect protocol JSON error body, but the browser receives ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR instead. This suggests the response is being malformed at the HTTP/2 frame level — possibly the content-length doesn't match, or the HTTP/2 stream is being improperly terminated. The Kimi frontend's error handling doesn't catch this network error — it silently drops the request.
details.comparison_with_382: Same silent failure as #382 (openai_compat_sse template). The kimi_connect template change did NOT resolve the issue. The problem is at the HTTP/2 protocol level, not the response content format.
details.recommendation: The kimi_connect template needs to ensure proper HTTP/2 frame handling: (1) correct content-length matching response body, (2) proper HEADERS frame with :status 400, (3) clean DATA frame with JSON body, (4) proper END_STREAM. Alternatively, try h2_mode=2 (keepalive) instead of goaway to see if the stream stays alive long enough for the error response to be read.

notes: Kimi block retest with kimi_connect template: BLOCKED but SILENT FAILURE persists. ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR in console. No visible error/warning in UI. The Connect protocol error format (400 + JSON) is not reaching the browser properly — HTTP/2 frame-level issue. Same outcome as #382.
